title: Hebrew gematria mod-9 identity with QA A1 axiom

claims.label: Aiq Bekar (Nine Chambers) is identically QA mod-9
Digital root is additive and multiplicative homomorphism

witnesses.vedic_square_identity: mod-9 multiplication table with 0 replaced by 9

claims.label: Three enneads: 27 signs = {mod-9 residue} x {decimal order}

witnesses.structure: 9 equivalence classes under mod-9, each with 3 members (units, tens, hundreds)
witnesses.convergence: Multiple independent alphabets converge on 9k signs (27, 28, 36)
witnesses.pythmen: Ancient Greek term for digital root; practiced by Pythagoreans

claims.label: Sefer Yetzirah computes 4! = 24 as structural constant

witnesses.qa_connection: 24 = min non-trivial Pisano fixed point AND max Carmichael lambda=2 modulus; pi(9)=24

claims.label: Skinner metrological kernel built on powers of 9

witnesses.skinner_quote: Adam, and the woman, and the serpent, are one in the center of this garden, as the number 9
witnesses.source: Skinner, Key to the Hebrew-Egyptian Mystery in the Source of Measures, 1875

claims.label: Factor 6 bridges mod-9 theoretical to mod-24 applied

witnesses.qa_pisano: pi(9) = 24; 24 / 9 mediated by factor structure involving 6

claims.label: Pythagorean transmission path documented via Iamblichus

witnesses.claim: Pythagoras learned number theory in Canaan, spent time at Mount Carmel
witnesses.mount_carmel: Center for religious/mystical retreats since Prophet Elijah (900-849 BCE)
witnesses.tetractys_sefirot: 1+2+3+4 = 10 may represent both Tetractys and Ten Sefirot

claims.label: Pre-biblical base-9 hypothesis: 9 as cognitive/computational optimum

witnesses.anomaly: Biblical Hebrew writes 15 as 9+6 and 16 as 9+7 (not 10+5, 10+6)
witnesses.ternary_optimality: Base 3 minimizes radix economy (Knuth); 9 = 3^2 is human-scale compression
witnesses.miller_law: 9 items = upper bound of human working memory (Miller 1956)

falsifiers.description: If Aiq Bekar mapped to {0,...,8} instead of {1,...,9}, it would violate A1 and break the identity
falsifiers.check: digital_root(n) must never return 0 for any positive integer

sources: Skinner, Key to the Hebrew-Egyptian Mystery in the Source of Measures (1875)
